title: Internet-facing asset × unpatched critical CVE
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate internal hosts (covered by 22.43.1 in aggregate).
evidence: Saved search 'internet_facing_critical_cve' (hourly) intersects external-facing inventory with unpatched critical CVEs present on any such host.

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 risk-management measures include vulnerability handling on exposed assets.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) (Security in acquisition, development and maintenance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.43.3: Internet-facing asset × unpatched critical CVE.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 6.3.3 requires critical vulnerabilities be addressed within one month; Internet-facing is the worst case.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI-DSS 6.3.3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.43.3: Internet-facing asset × unpatched critical CVE.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.43.3' running on Vulnerability scanner feeds, external-facing inventory (Shodan, Censys, AWS ALB/NLB, cloud LBs)., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: RA-5 vulnerability scanning and remediation; Internet-facing is a priority.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST-800-53 RA-5 (Vulnerability scanning) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.43.3: Internet-facing asset × unpatched critical CVE.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.43.3' running on Vulnerability scanner feeds, external-facing inventory (Shodan, Censys, AWS ALB/NLB, cloud LBs)., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Host web-01 is in the external-facing inventory and has CVE-2024-XXXX (CVSS 9.8) unpatched for 3 days; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Host is on internal network; no alert (different UC handles internal).

dataSources: Vulnerability scanner feeds, external-facing inventory (Shodan, Censys, AWS ALB/NLB, cloud LBs).
spl:
index=vuln earliest=-24h cvss_v3_base>=9.0
| lookup external_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT external_exposed
| where external_exposed="true"
| stats min(_time) as first_seen values(cve) as cves by host
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_seen)/86400,0)
| where age_days>0
| table host cves age_days
description: Any Internet-facing asset with an unpatched critical CVE is the single highest-priority audit and security finding; this UC keeps it at one-hour latency.
value: Eliminates the gap between scanner output and risk-based patch-SLA tracking.
implementation: (1) Maintain external_asset_inventory.csv refreshed daily from LB config or external attack-surface tooling; (2) run hourly; (3) SOAR auto-escalates on hit. Indexes required: `index=vuln`.
visualization: Table of Internet-facing hosts with critical CVEs, bar chart of patch-age buckets, single-value 'Internet-facing hosts fully patched'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application

knownFalsePositives: SLA breaches during compatibility testing, vendor lag, or planned change windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Vulnerability scanner feeds, external-facing inventory (Shodan, Censys, AWS ALB/NLB, cloud LBs)..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain external_asset_inventory.csv refreshed daily from LB config or external attack-surface tooling; (2) run hourly; (3) SOAR auto-escalates on hit.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vuln earliest=-24h cvss_v3_base>=9.0
| lookup external_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT external_exposed
| where external_exposed="true"
| stats min(_time) as first_seen values(cve) as cves by host
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_seen)/86400,0)
| where age_days>0
| table host cves age_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Internet-facing asset × unpatched critical CVE** — Eliminates the gap between scanner output and risk-based patch-SLA tracking.

Documented **Data sources**: Vulnerability scanner feeds, external-facing inventory (Shodan, Censys, AWS ALB/NLB, cloud LBs). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vuln.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vuln, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where external_exposed="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **host**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: age_days, cves, first_seen, host.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with patch or vulnerability management tooling and compare with the GRC system so SLAs match the agreed control timeline.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of Internet-facing hosts with critical CVEs, bar chart of patch-age buckets, single-value 'Internet-facing hosts fully patched'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see patch timing against your service promises so you can explain delays with evidence.
